footer.tagline: Building the future of intelligent client solutions.

home.problem_title: IT outages are costing enterprises and public agencies
home.problem_desc: IT incidents in the financial sector keep rising, yet existing tools lack dedicated endpoint performance and incident analysis. IT admins field constant PC performance and outage complaints with no systematic answer.
home.problem_p2: A solution that detects IT issues in real time and analyzes root causes automatically is needed.
home.p1_desc: Fast detection, root-cause analysis, real-time performance monitoring, and rapid incident response maximize IT operations.
home.p2_desc: Accurate root-cause analysis with trusted data, and precise visibility into software and hardware interactions.
home.p3_desc: Widget-based flexible layouts, real-time 1:1 and 1:N remote commands, and a low-overhead architecture support stable operations.
home.kf1_desc: Continuously collects CPU · memory · I/O · handle load at the kernel level, with time-series trend analysis.
home.kf2_desc: Auto-detects symptoms like abnormal termination, BSOD, and CPU spikes, tracing root cause, entry path, and enterprise-wide impact.
home.kf3_desc: Monitors a specific PC's performance and processes in real time, with live response options.
home.kf4_desc: Broadcasts commands to every PC and aggregates results in real time. Combine commands freely to build any scenario.
home.usecase_1_desc: Compare resource usage and symptom trends across PCs before and after a rollout.
home.usecase_2_desc: Track performance and symptom shifts before and after patch deployment, by department and PC.
home.usecase_3_desc: Inspect process and driver state at the moment the symptom occurred, in real time.
home.usecase_4_desc: Query versions across all PCs and collect results in bulk.
home.usecase_5_title: Remote recovery of a lost PC password
home.usecase_5_desc: Remotely restore the account state on the PC.
home.usecase_6_desc: Query storage lifespan across the fleet and pick replacement candidates.
home.usecase_7_desc: Query USB storage connection history across all PCs for outflow auditing.
home.usecase_8_desc: Collect accounts in the Administrators group across the fleet to uncover unauthorized privileges.
home.usecase_9_desc: Instantly spot PCs with real-time protection off or outdated definitions.
home.usecase_10_desc: Immediately identify PCs missing a specific KB update, and select deployment targets.
home.usecase_11_desc: Send maintenance, patch, and training notices to every PC as tray notifications, instantly.
home.usecase_12_desc: Over 60 inspection and response templates ship built-in — add any scenario your field needs.
home.partners_desc: Trusted by enterprises and public agencies in Korea
home.cta_title: Raise the ease and safety of IT management
home.cta_desc: See real endpoint visibility with an Orange The Client live demo.

agent.title: Agent
agent.desc: An autonomous AI agent that understands your goals and executes complex workflows end-to-end — without constant human supervision.
agent.cap1_desc: Interprets complex instructions and converts them into actionable steps.
agent.cap2_desc: Chains multiple tasks together to complete end-to-end processes autonomously.
agent.cap3_desc: Adjusts its behavior dynamically based on live data and changing conditions.
agent.cap4_desc: Runs in isolated environments to prevent unauthorized data access.
agent.cap5_desc: Connects seamlessly with your existing tools, APIs, and data sources.
agent.cap6_desc: Full logging of every action taken for compliance and debugging.
agent.cta_title: Ready to deploy your first Agent?
agent.cta_desc: Connect with our team to get started with a proof of concept for your use case.

manager.title: Manager
manager.desc: The command center for your entire AI agent ecosystem. Observe, control, and optimize every automation from one powerful dashboard.
manager.feat1_desc: Monitor all agents, tasks, and workflows from a single unified interface.
manager.feat2_desc: Fine-grained permissions to control who can view, edit, or deploy agents.
manager.feat3_desc: Live status updates, performance metrics, and alert notifications.
manager.feat4_desc: Visual drag-and-drop editor for designing complex automation pipelines.
manager.feat5_desc: Pre-built integrations with major enterprise data sources and cloud services.
manager.feat6_desc: Detailed reports on agent performance, cost, and business impact.
manager.cta_desc: See how Manager can streamline operations across your organization.

introduction.title: Introduction to Orange The Client
introduction.desc: A technical guide for first-time users of Orange The Client. Walk through system components, core concepts, and deployment procedures step by step.
introduction.component_agent_desc: Installed on each endpoint (desktop, laptop, ATM, kiosk) to handle real-time data collection and event detection. Connects to the Kernel via IPC and communicates with the Manager over a persistent TCP connection.
introduction.component_kernel_desc: Monitors process behavior inside the OS using an Event Driven approach. Information is collected only when events occur, adding no extra load to the user's PC.
introduction.component_manager_desc: A web-based dashboard for administrators. Provides full operational capabilities including real-time monitoring, fault analysis, remote command execution, and report generation.
introduction.component_console_desc: Administrators can monitor all endpoints and execute remote commands in 1:1 or 1:N mode.
introduction.concept_1_desc: Unlike the periodic polling used by competing products, information is collected directly from within the OS only when events occur. This enables real-time detection of performance changes with zero additional overhead.
introduction.concept_2_desc: Supports the latest security paradigm that treats every endpoint as a potential threat. Only authorized devices (users) are granted access through continuous monitoring.
introduction.concept_3_desc: Short for Summarized Unique ID — a unique identifier that quantifies process behavior by combining execution argument patterns, file hashes, parent processes, and more. Enables tracking of identically behaving processes across different PCs.
introduction.step_1: Contact the sales team to request the product and discuss the installation environment
introduction.step_2: Set up the Orange The Client server at the customer site
introduction.step_3: Download, install, and run the Agent from the landing page on each PC
introduction.step_4: Verify the node widget in the Manager
introduction.specs_cpu_rec: Intel Core i5 or equivalent and above
introduction.next_manual_desc: Explore detailed feature descriptions and operational guides.
introduction.next_blog_desc: Read real-world implementation cases and fault analysis tips.

userManual.title: Orange The Client Manual
userManual.desc: Quick Guide for Enterprise Administrators — Orange The Client ver 1.6. Covers server operating environment, Manager usage, and Agent installation.
userManual.toc_overview_desc: Describes the purpose of Orange The Client and the role of the server.
userManual.toc_server_desc: Covers hardware specs, network requirements, and firewall configuration.
userManual.toc_manager_desc: Explains how to access the web-based management console and use each section.
userManual.toc_agent_desc: Covers installation, updates, removal, and detection rules for the endpoint agent.
userManual.overview_desc: Orange The Client monitors endpoint behavior to detect performance and fault-related issues and provides contextual information. The server delivers Manager to enterprise administrators and maintains a persistent TCP connection with agents installed on user PCs, enabling real-time bidirectional communication even when PCs are behind NAT.
userManual.network_desc: Please provide the following network information to OrangeLabs before server installation.
userManual.manager_access_desc: Open a web browser and navigate to the Manager server address. Click the [Manager] link on the landing page, or append /manager to the URL for direct access.
userManual.manager_login_desc: Enter the Host (REST API server IP:port), administrator ID, and password to log in. Upon successful login, Host and ID are saved in the browser.
userManual.manager_sec_dashboard_desc: Visualizes company-wide node load, symptom summaries, node status, and network anomalies to proactively identify fault indicators.
userManual.manager_sec_node_desc: Displays a grid of all PCs with the agent installed. Filter by online/offline status or load level. Manage nodes by department via the org tree.
userManual.manager_sec_symptom_desc: View symptom data by time period in word cloud charts and grids. Drill down through symptom levels to inspect detailed history.
userManual.manager_sec_load_desc: Displays node load history — CPU, Memory, I/O, and Handle — as candlestick charts with live updates and prior-day comparison.
userManual.manager_sec_daily_desc: Provides hourly symptom statistics and day-over-day trends as charts. Export PDF/Excel reports for efficient fault tracking.
userManual.manager_sec_command_desc: Execute real-time commands on agents and review results. Supports DB queries and WMI-based commands. Save command patterns as templates for reuse.
userManual.manager_sec_report_desc: Manage daily new symptom reports by date. View total new symptoms, affected nodes, BSODs, and high-risk nodes with day-over-day change indicators.
userManual.manager_sec_rule_desc: Manage detection rules across 14 categories including BSOD, abnormal termination, and processes. Enable or disable individual rules as needed.
userManual.manager_sec_org_desc: Manage the company structure as a tree. Assign PCs to departments and users via the node assignment popup.
userManual.manager_sec_setting_desc: Modify system-wide and administrator account settings including Manager server address and options affecting agents, servers, and Manager.
userManual.agent_intro: The Agent is downloaded from the server and installed on Windows OS. Communication between the Agent and server uses RESTAPI (SSL) and TCP (SSL).
userManual.agent_install_desc: The installer filename follows the format ORANAGE.x64_[ServerIP]_[Port].exe. Use the /S flag for silent installation without a UI.
userManual.agent_update_desc: After initial deployment via PMS, the agent receives continuous updates through the built-in distribution system. The installed version is visible in the Manager node list.
userManual.agent_delete_desc: Users can uninstall via Windows Add/Remove Programs, or administrators can remotely uninstall using the @uninstall command through Manager Live CMD.
userManual.agent_detect_desc: Detects symptoms using a variety of detection rules built on core capabilities. Rules are continuously added and updated; the latest rules can be viewed in Manager's Symptom Rule Management section.
userManual.agent_tray_desc: After installation, a tray icon appears in the Windows system tray. The icon color changes from green → yellow → red → brown based on CPU usage. Hover to see a tooltip with CPU, Memory, I/O, and Handle stats.

about.hero_desc: OrangeLabs develops endpoint performance and fault management systems that meet the standards of enterprise IT administrators — solving problems that security products alone cannot.
about.mission_title: What Every IT Admin Needs — But Security Products Can't Solve
about.mission_p1: Administrative PCs rely on many government-dedicated programs. Service failures caused by software conflicts or Windows patches are common — and far more frequent than security incidents.
about.mission_p2: If endpoint data could help identify root causes of failures, it would be enormously valuable for IT operations. That insight is what drove the founding of OrangeLabs.
about.product_bullet1: Kernel-level event-driven data collection — no polling lag
about.product_bullet2: Real-time anomaly detection across all endpoints simultaneously
about.product_bullet3: AI-powered BSOD and crash dump analysis
about.product_tagline: Next-generation endpoint performance and fault management solution powered by AI
about.group_desc: Four group companies collaborate organically to deliver complete services — from product development to sales, security consulting, and maintenance.
about.group_c1_desc: IT comprehensive consulting & construction, sole distributor of The Client (domestic & international)
about.group_c2_desc: New solution R&D, The Client manufacturer
about.group_c3_desc: Security specialized consulting & construction, The Client security advisory
about.group_c4_desc: Nationwide maintenance, The Client maintenance support
about.ceo_desc: Designed the structure of endpoint performance and fault management solutions that enterprise IT administrators need, and founded OrangeLabs.
about.arch_sub: From hardware to console — complete coverage at every level of your endpoint environment.
about.arch1_desc: Direct hardware-level telemetry — CPU, memory, I/O, network adapters
about.arch2_desc: Proprietary Orange Kernel Driver captures OS events in real time without polling
about.arch3_desc: Lightweight always-on agent with process lineage tracking (SUID) and policy enforcement
about.arch4_desc: Unified management console for cross-endpoint correlation, dashboards, and remote control
about.cap_title: What Orange The Client Can Do
about.cap1_desc: Event-driven collection at the OS kernel level using our proprietary Kernel Driver — true real-time, not polling-based.
about.cap2_desc: Automatic BSOD and application crash dump collection with AI-driven root cause analysis across all endpoints.
about.cap3_desc: Unique SUID-based process identification tracks every process lifecycle and execution lineage across your fleet.
about.cap4_desc: Aggregate, compare, and correlate events — from Windows Updates to security incidents — across your entire fleet simultaneously.
about.cap5_desc: Real-time 1:1 and 1:N remote command execution directly on target endpoints without requiring admin privileges.
about.cap6_desc: Built for GDPR, CCPA, and Korean Personal Information Protection Act compliance from the ground up.
about.vp1_desc: Reduce IT operational overhead by automating endpoint monitoring, anomaly detection, and incident response workflows.
about.vp2_desc: Accelerate incident resolution with real-time data, AI-driven root cause analysis, and instant remote command execution.
about.vp3_desc: Elevate your IT support quality with proactive monitoring, pre-collected crash data, and granular endpoint intelligence.

support.title: Support
support.desc: From onboarding to ongoing operations, OrangeLabs is with you every step of the way.
support.docs_desc: Browse our comprehensive docs and guides.
support.contact_desc: Submit a ticket and get a response within 4 hours.
support.onboard_desc: Schedule a session with our customer success team.
support.faq_1_a: Orange The Client is an AI-powered next-generation endpoint performance and fault management solution developed by OrangeLabs. It provides real-time performance monitoring and automated root cause analysis for enterprise and public sector endpoint devices such as laptops, desktop PCs, ATMs, and kiosks, enhancing the efficiency and safety of IT administrators.
support.faq_2_q: How is it different from existing IT management solutions?
support.faq_2_a: Existing solutions were centered around infrastructure such as servers and networks, with virtually no dedicated endpoint performance and fault management solutions available. Orange The Client comprehensively provides features unavailable in competing products, including software conflict analysis, S/W-H/W impact analysis, and kernel-level real-time monitoring. Notably, its machine learning and AI-based fault prediction capability is unique in the industry.
support.faq_3_q: How much does fault resolution time decrease after implementation?
support.faq_3_a: Before implementation, fault recognition took approximately 30 minutes and fault analysis and resolution took 1 week to 1 month. After implementation, faults are recognized instantly with real-time quantified analysis data, allowing fault resolution within 4 hours. Fault reports are also generated immediately instead of taking 1 week.
support.faq_4_q: How is AI technology used in the product?
support.faq_4_a: The Agent detects fault symptoms in real time, collects and analyzes information, and identifies the root cause. AI automatically suggests the optimal remediation for each symptom. Machine learning enables proactive fault prediction, and AI-based analysis of S/W and H/W impact enables detection of complex faults such as solution conflicts.
support.faq_5_q: Does it impact user PC performance?
support.faq_5_a: It is designed to minimize impact. Orange The Client uses its proprietary Kernel Driver to collect information only when events occur within the OS using an Event Driven approach. Unlike the periodic polling method of competing products, it collects performance information in real time without additional overhead, resulting in minimal impact on the user PC environment.
support.faq_6_a: With its lightweight architecture comprising Agent, Manager, Kernel, and Console, it can be deployed across various infrastructure environments including on-premises, hybrid, and cloud. A flexible widget-based dashboard and real-time 1:1 and 1:N remote command (Live Command) features enable centralized management of all endpoint devices from a single screen.
support.faq_7_q: What kind of company is OrangeLabs?
support.faq_7_a: OrangeLabs Co., Ltd. is a solution research and development company founded in November 2023 and the manufacturer of Orange The Client. Led by CEO Bumjin Kim, an expert in IB2B system architecture, the company launched its prototype in 2024, obtained GS certification (Grade 1), and is currently registering with the Korea Online E-Procurement System. OrangeSys handles sales and deployment, OrangeSecu provides security advisory, and OrangeInfotech manages maintenance.

otc.hero_desc: Next-generation endpoint performance and fault management solution powered by AI. From real-time detection to AI-driven root cause analysis — elevating IT convenience and stability.
otc.problem_title: Economic Losses from IT Failures in Enterprises & Public Institutions
otc.problem_desc: IT incidents in the financial sector continue to rise. Existing solutions lack dedicated endpoint performance and fault analysis capabilities. IT admins receive countless queries about PC slowdowns and failures, yet have no systematic solution.
otc.problem_p2: A solution that detects IT problems in real-time and automatically analyzes root causes is essential.
otc.prob2_title: The Difficulty of Detecting PC Failures
otc.prob2_p1: IT administrators first learn of failures through individual PC user reports
otc.prob2_p2: Because root cause identification is difficult, the first step is to consult the primary security solution vendor
otc.prob2_p3: If unresolved, all currently deployed solution vendors are contacted (secondary response)
otc.prob2_p4: When new solutions or OS updates are introduced, lack of baseline data forces vendors to coordinate and identify root causes through mutual consultation
otc.prob3_title: Manual Fault Analysis Processes Reduce IT Operational Efficiency
otc.prob3_col1_p1: As productivity and security both become critical, additional programs are installed and frequently updated to match the environment
otc.prob3_col1_p2: Increasing number of installed programs leads to PC performance degradation and inter-program conflicts causing more failures
otc.prob3_col1_highlight: The root cause resolution process relies entirely on manual work
otc.prob3_col2_p1: Resolving PC performance issues requires involvement from internal administrators all the way to external developers of each program
otc.prob3_col2_p2: Complete absence of before/after comparison data when new programs are installed makes accurate diagnosis impossible
otc.prob3_col2_p3: When the same issue recurs, the entire manual process must be repeated from scratch
otc.prob3_col2_highlight: This results in significant time, manpower, and cost expenditure
otc.prob3_result: Extensive time required due to various tests and inter-vendor conflicts to identify root causes
otc.chart_source: Source: Financial Supervisory Service「Current Status of Financial Sector IT Failures (2022)」
otc.overview_title: Endpoint Management in a Zero Trust Environment
otc.ep_col1_desc: End-user devices connected to the network for information exchange (laptops, desktops, smartphones, ATMs, medical terminals, etc.)
otc.ep_col2_b1_desc: Acts as the boundary between internal and external systems — a key channel for malware infection, data breaches, and other security incidents
otc.ep_col2_b2_desc: In the Zero Trust paradigm, all endpoints are treated as potential threats, allowing access only to authenticated devices through continuous monitoring
otc.ep_col2_b3_desc: As regulations such as GDPR, CCPA, and Korea's Personal Information Protection Act are tightened, endpoint data protection has become a legal obligation
otc.ep_col3_title: Need for Endpoint Performance & Fault Management
otc.ep_col3_b1: Many resources running continuously and periodically for networking, computing, and security require systematic endpoint performance and fault management
otc.ep_col3_b2: Government policy mandates performance and fault management for stable endpoint operations to improve information system uptime and digital service quality
otc.comp_b1: Agent, Manager, Kernel, and Console are always connected over the network (low-load collection architecture)
otc.comp_b2: Automated behavior-based data collection and analysis (machine learning technology for problem prediction)
otc.comp_b3: Applicable to various service environments including Premise, On-Premise, and Cloud
otc.arch_desc: Agent, Manager, Kernel, and Console are always connected over the network in a low-load collection architecture. Applicable to Premise, On-Premise, and Cloud environments.
otc.arch1_desc: Installed on each endpoint (desktop, laptop, ATM, kiosk) to handle real-time data collection and event detection. Connects to the Kernel via IPC and communicates with the Manager over a persistent TCP connection.
otc.arch2_desc: Monitors process behavior inside the OS using an Event Driven approach. Information is collected only when events occur, adding no extra load to the user's PC.
otc.arch3_desc: A web-based dashboard for administrators. Provides full operational capabilities including real-time monitoring, fault analysis, remote command execution, and report generation.
otc.arch4_desc: Administrators can monitor all endpoints and execute remote commands in 1:1 or 1:N mode.
otc.feat_card_spd1_title: Fast Fault Resolution Through Real-Time Detection and Root Cause Analysis
otc.feat_card_spd2_title: Ensuring User PC Stability Through Real-Time Performance Monitoring
otc.feat_card_spd2_p1: Real-time screen display (user status, performance, faults) via Push Notification technology in the Manager UI
otc.feat_card_spd2_p2: Real-time PC management via real-time networking between Manager UI – Manager and Agent – Manager
otc.feat_card1_title: Fault Analysis Technology Secured Through Diverse Development Experience
otc.feat_card1_type1_desc: Continuous installation failure messages during the program installation process
otc.feat_card1_type2_title: Frequent Termination of Newly Introduced Programs
otc.feat_card1_type2_desc: Frequent terminations of newly installed programs, unresolvable even by the program manufacturer
otc.feat_card1_type3_desc: Executable files of essential programs disappear → disappear again even after reinstalling
otc.feat_card1_sol1_desc: Security solution's new file analysis → file pre-emption, causing interference in the installation process
otc.feat_card1_sol2_desc: Security solution malfunction or misconfiguration — forcing program termination
otc.feat_card1_sol3_desc: Security solution malfunction or misconfiguration — causing forced file deletion
otc.feat_card1_summary_title: Applying System Management Development & Multi-Enterprise Operation Know-how
otc.feat_card1_summary_p1: Solutions from engineers involved in all areas of system management development and design
otc.feat_card1_summary_p2: Experience analyzing various security solutions + behavioral patterns of general products
otc.feat_card1_summary_p3: Endpoint behavior data collection technology + information linkage + deep understanding of causality and impact analysis know-how
otc.feat_card1_result1_bold: complex failures entangled with various issues
otc.feat_card1_result2_bold: general performance metrics like CPU/memory cannot resolve
otc.feat_card2_p1: Collecting and providing evidence data (Dump File) by symptom type, frequency, time, user count, cause, and manufacturer for root cause resolution
otc.feat_card2_p2: Real-time symptom analysis and solution suggestions using AI
otc.feat_card2_ai_title: AI Technology Aligned with the National AI Digital Innovation Policy
otc.feat_card2_ai_p1: Agent detects fault symptoms in real-time, collects information, analyzes, and identifies root causes
otc.feat_card2_ai_p2: AI automatically suggests optimal remediation for each detected symptom
otc.feat_card2_result2: AI-based analysis of solution conflicts and impact
otc.feat_card3_title: Proactive Fault Prevention Through Real-Time Integrated Misconfiguration Management
otc.feat_card3_p1: Proactive prevention of duplicate Hostname / IP configurations
otc.feat_card3_p2: Proactive prevention of issues caused by duplicate use of security solutions and network adapters
otc.feat_card4_title: Automated Data Aggregation and Real-Time Processing Architecture
otc.feat_card4_p1: 1:N command delivery and information aggregation targeting all PCs
otc.feat_card4_p2: Command delivery, remote commands, information aggregation, and agent detail streaming for a specific single PC
otc.f1_desc: 24/7 monitoring with a flexible widget-based dashboard, configurable per department and role.
otc.f2_desc: View CPU, Memory, I/O, and Handle load by day, month, and graph format. Query performance at any point in time.
otc.f3_desc: Detect abnormal termination, forced closure, CPU spikes, and BSODs — with AI-powered root cause analysis by symptom type, user, frequency, and date.
otc.f4_desc: View symptoms and users by day/hour. Events, symptom types, and root causes are displayed separately.
otc.f5_desc: Execute 1:N commands across all enterprise PCs. Remotely operate Windows CMD in real-time for any targeted endpoint.
otc.f6_desc: Real-time symptom analysis and resolution recommendations via AI. Supports ML-based failure prediction and software collision impact analysis.
otc.mgr_title: Fast Situation Awareness with an Intuitive Management Screen
otc.mgr_desc: Various widget-based management screens intuitively provide real-time status information, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, 1:N remote commands, and more.
otc.mgr1_c2: Real-time status information provided via status widgets
otc.mgr1_c3: Anomaly detection through comparison with previous data
otc.mgr2_c1: Real-time monitoring of all PCs with agents installed
otc.mgr2_c2: View node info, status, and performance metrics at a glance
otc.mgr2_c3: Quickly analyze root cause and impact through detailed node information
otc.mgr3_c1: Review various symptom detection results including abnormal termination and BSOD
otc.mgr3_c2: Detailed analysis of node info, frequency, and time zone related to symptoms
otc.mgr3_c3: AI-based automatic root cause analysis and resolution suggestions per symptom
otc.mgr4_c1: Visualize load generated by nodes per time period
otc.mgr4_c2: Detect anomalies by comparing yesterday's and today's load patterns
otc.mgr4_c3: Quickly identify high-load history through charts
otc.mgr5_c1: View symptom types and frequency for the day at a glance
otc.mgr5_c2: Visualize concentrated symptom patterns per time period to detect anomalies
otc.mgr5_c3: Analyze root causes by querying detailed history of a designated time period
otc.mgr6_c1: Issue and process commands to designated nodes via templated commands
otc.mgr6_c2: Collect and analyze various node information optimized for PC management
otc.mgr6_c3: Respond to various situations with flexible and highly extensible command templates
otc.impact_title: How Incident Response Time Changes After Deployment
otc.impact_r2_note: Instant visibility of scope and targets
otc.compare_key_desc: No competitor uses machine learning and AI-based technology to predict failures.
otc.cta_desc: See what true endpoint visibility looks like with a live demo of Orange The Client.
otc.cmp_item_4: Root cause analysis of software conflicts

demoVideos.pageDesc: Watch key features of Orange The Client in action.
demoVideos.v1_desc:
The dashboard is the first screen you see when you access the Orange The Client Manager.
Widgets containing various information are arranged on the screen, providing a dynamic view by receiving real-time data.
The widgets are designed to give you an at-a-glance overview of all key information.
demoVideos.v2_desc:
Orange The Client includes an Agent, and any PC with the Agent installed is defined as a node.
It provides a visualized list of nodes along with real-time and detailed information for each node.
demoVideos.v3_title: Orange The Client Manager - Live CMD
demoVideos.v3_desc: Via the Agent installed on the PC, you can access the command prompt of that PC and check detailed information.
demoVideos.v4_desc:
Collects and summarizes symptoms and errors occurring on nodes, allowing you to review all events within a specified period. You can drill down into symptom details to ultimately view in-depth information about a specific symptom.
It provides detailed information including the cause, result, time of occurrence, and resolution of each symptom.
demoVideos.v5_title: Orange The Client Manager - Node Load
demoVideos.v5_desc: Collects CPU, Memory, I/O, and Handle load data from all nodes as time-series data and visualizes it through charts. Load information such as peak, minimum, and range is displayed on charts by time period, and a detailed history list shows the reasons behind load spikes at any given point.
demoVideos.v6_title: Orange The Client Manager - Daily Symptoms
demoVideos.v6_desc: Visualizes a full day's worth of symptom history. Graphs of symptom occurrences for each of the 24 hourly periods, alongside the previous day's history, are provided so you can easily compare yesterday's and today's records.
demoVideos.v7_desc: Send real-time commands to all (or selected) nodes to collect actual information such as hardware, software, symptoms, and asset data. Commands can be sent immediately or scheduled for a predetermined time, and a variety of built-in templates alone enable extensive information gathering.
demoVideos.v8_desc:
Reports on the previous day's symptoms and errors are automatically generated every day in the early morning hours.
Lists of nodes with high load or frequent symptoms, as well as key symptom summaries, are provided along with PDF download support for use as formal reports.
Each item in the report contains detailed information, so you can click any item to view its full details.
demoVideos.v9_title: Orange The Client Manager - Symptom Rules
demoVideos.v9_desc:
You can configure the rules for symptoms and errors that the node's Agent will collect.
In addition to toggling each rule on or off, you can also view a numeric count of how many symptoms have been detected for each individual rule.
demoVideos.v10_title: Orange The Client Manager - Widget Layout Editor
demoVideos.v10_desc: Widgets can be arranged in a layout that is most convenient for the administrator. Tabs for placing widgets can be added or removed, and widgets can be dragged from the widget list and dropped into any desired position.
